mission.title: submariner: Add dependency license scanning linting
mission.description: Add dependency license scanning linting. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current submariner deployment
steps.description:
Verify your submariner version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n submariner -l app.kubernetes.io/name=submariner
helm list -n submariner 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working submariner installation.

steps.title: Review submariner configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant submariner configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n submariner -l app.kubernetes.io/name=submariner
kubectl get configmap -n submariner -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=submariner
```
**What would you like to be added**:

License scanning paralleling the CNCF periodic license scanning, to make sure that all of our code and dependencies use a CNCF-approved license.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add dependency license scanning linting
steps.description:
The [CNCF application process](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1bJhG1MuM981uQXcnBMv4Mj9yfV5_q5Kwk3qhBCLa_5A) says we need to follow [CNCF IP rules](https://github.com/cncf/foundation/blob/master/charter.md#11-ip-policy), which in turn say:

I guess that means we need to be confident that all of our dependencies use OSI-approved licenses.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n submariner -l app.kubernetes.io/name=submariner
kubectl get events -n submariner --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add dependency license scanning linting" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
The [CNCF application process](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1bJhG1MuM981uQXcnBMv4Mj9yfV5_q5Kwk3qhBCLa_5A) says we need to follow [CNCF IP rules](https://github.com/cncf/foundation/blob/master/charter.md#11-ip-policy), which in turn say:

I guess that means we need to be confident that all of our dependencies use OSI-approved licenses.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with submariner installed or the issue environment reproducible.
